texto: Everyone was eating, talking softly, glancing at me, hugging me, eating. It was as if someone had turned the volume down. Everything looked normal, but the sound was muted. Death did this, set all this weirdness in motion, made people appear out of nowhere carrying casseroles, saying 'I'm sorry' over and over, death muffled their voices.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Joan Abelove: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Everyone was eating, talking softly, glancing at me, hugging me, eating. It was as if someone had…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, death, funerals pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e funerals.
